The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

2. Not to enter on privet land without consent.-

Provided always,ï¿½ that nothing herein shall authorize or empower the said Company to lay down orï¿½ place anyï¿½ pipe orï¿½ other works into, through, or against any building, orï¿½ in anyï¿½ land notï¿½ dedicated toï¿½ public use,ï¿½ without the consent ofï¿½ the ownersï¿½ and occupiersï¿½ thereof; exceptï¿½ that the said Company mayï¿½ at anyï¿½ time enterï¿½ upon and lay or place any new pipe inï¿½ the placeï¿½ of anï¿½ existing pipe, in any land wherein any pipe has been already lawfully laid down or placed in pursuance of this Act, and mayï¿½ repair or alter any pipe so laid down.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

3. Noticeï¿½ to beï¿½ served onï¿½ persons having control, etc., beforeï¿½ breaking upï¿½ streetsï¿½ orï¿½ openingï¿½ drains.-

Before theï¿½ said Companyï¿½ proceed toï¿½ open orï¿½ break upï¿½ and street,ï¿½ bridge, sewer,ï¿½ drain, orï¿½ tunnel, they shall give to the Municipal Commissioners for the Town of Calcutta, orï¿½ other persons under whose control or management the same may be,ï¿½ or toï¿½ their Clerk,ï¿½ Surveyor, orï¿½ other officer,ï¿½ noticeï¿½ in writing of their intention to open or break up the same, not less thanï¿½ three clearï¿½ days beforeï¿½ beginning suchï¿½ work;ï¿½ exceptï¿½ inï¿½ casesï¿½ ofï¿½ emergency arising from defects in any of the pipes or other works, and then soï¿½ soon asï¿½ is possibleï¿½ after the beginning of the work, or the necessity for the same shall have arisen.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

4.ï¿½ Streetsï¿½ï¿½ orï¿½ drainsï¿½ï¿½ notï¿½ toï¿½ beï¿½ brokenï¿½ upï¿½ exceptï¿½ under superintendence ofï¿½ persons havingï¿½ control ofï¿½ the same.ï¿½

Ifï¿½ persons having theï¿½ control, etc.,ï¿½ fail toï¿½ superintend, companyï¿½ may proceed with theï¿½ works.--No suchï¿½ street, bridge,ï¿½ sewer,ï¿½ drain,ï¿½ orï¿½ tunnel shall, except in the cases of emergency aforesaid, be opened or broken up, except under the superintendence of the persons having the control or management thereof, or of their officer, and according to such plan as shallï¿½ be approvedï¿½ of by such persons or their officer, or in case of anyï¿½ difference respecting such plan, then according to such plan as shall beï¿½ determined byï¿½ a Magistrate; and such Magistrate may, on the application ofï¿½ the personsï¿½ having theï¿½ control orï¿½ management of any such sewerï¿½ or drain,ï¿½ or theirï¿½ officer, requireï¿½ the said Company to make suchï¿½ temporary orï¿½ other worksï¿½ as theyï¿½ may think necessary for guarding against any interruption of the drainage during the execution of any works which interfere with any such sewer or drain;

Provided alwaysï¿½ that, ifï¿½ the personsï¿½ havingï¿½ suchï¿½ controlï¿½ or management as aforesaid, and their officer, fail to attend at the time fixed forï¿½ the openingï¿½ of anyï¿½ such street,ï¿½ bridge, sewer, drain, or tunnel, afterï¿½ having hadï¿½ such notice of the said Company's intention as aforesaid, or shall not propose any plan for breaking up or opening the same, or shall refuse or neglect to superintend the operation, the said Company may perform the work specified in such notice without the superintendence of such persons or their officer.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

5. Streetsï¿½ broken upï¿½ to be reinstated with out delay.-

When the said Companyï¿½ open orï¿½ break upï¿½ the road or pavement of any street or bridge,ï¿½ orï¿½ anyï¿½ sewer,ï¿½ drain,ï¿½ orï¿½ tunnel,ï¿½ theyï¿½ shall,ï¿½ withï¿½ allï¿½ convenient speed, complete the work for which the same shall be broken up, andï¿½ fill inï¿½ the ground,ï¿½ and reinstate and make good the road or pavement, orï¿½ the sewer,ï¿½ drain, or tunnel so opened or broken up, and carry awayï¿½ the rubbishï¿½ occasioned thereby;ï¿½ and shallï¿½ at all times, whilst anyï¿½ such roadï¿½ or pavementï¿½ shall beï¿½ so openedï¿½ or broken up, cause theï¿½ same toï¿½ be fencedï¿½ and guarded,ï¿½ and shallï¿½ cause a light, sufficient for the warning of passengers, to be set up an 1 maintained against orï¿½ near such road or pavement where the same shall be open or broken up,ï¿½ every nightï¿½ during which the same shall be continued open or brokenï¿½ up; andï¿½ shall keepï¿½ the road or pavement which has been so broken upï¿½ in goodï¿½ repair for three months after replacing and making good theï¿½ same, and for such further time, if any, not being more than twelve monthsï¿½ in the whole, s the soil so broken up shall continue to subside.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

6. Penalty for delay in reinstating streets.-

If the said Company open or break up any street or bridge, or any sewer, drain, or tunnel, without giving such notice as aforesaid, or in a manner different from that whichï¿½ shall have been approved of or determined as aforesaid, or without makingï¿½ such temporaryï¿½ or otherï¿½ works asï¿½ aforesaid, when so required, exceptï¿½ in theï¿½ cases inï¿½ which theï¿½ said Company are hereby authorized toï¿½ performï¿½ suchï¿½ worksï¿½ withoutï¿½ anyï¿½ superintendenceï¿½ orï¿½ notice; orï¿½ if theï¿½ said Company make any delay in completing any suchï¿½ work, orï¿½ in fillingï¿½ in the ground or reinstating and making good the road orï¿½ pavement, orï¿½ the sewer, drain, or tunnel so opened or broken up, orï¿½ in carryingï¿½ away theï¿½ rubbish occasionedï¿½ thereby; or if they neglect to cause the place where such road or pavement has been broken up toï¿½ be fenced, guarded, and lighted, or neglect to keep the road or pavement inï¿½ repair forï¿½ the space of three months next after the same shall haveï¿½ been madeï¿½ good, orï¿½ such furtherï¿½ time as aforesaid, they shall forfeitï¿½ to theï¿½ persons having the control or management of the street, bridge,ï¿½ sewer, drain,ï¿½ or tunnel,ï¿½ in respectï¿½ of whichï¿½ such default isï¿½ made, aï¿½ sum notï¿½ exceeding fiftyï¿½ rupees forï¿½ everyï¿½ such offence, andï¿½ they shall forfeit an additional sum not exceeding fifty rupees forï¿½ each dayï¿½ during whichï¿½ any suchï¿½ delay as aforesaid shall continue after they shall have received notice thereof.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

7. In case of delay, other parties may reinstate, and recover the expenses. Expenseï¿½ how toï¿½ be ascertainedï¿½ and recovered.-

If any such delay orï¿½ omission asï¿½ aforesaid takeï¿½ place, theï¿½ persons havingï¿½ the control orï¿½ management of the street, bridge, sewer, drain, or tunnel, in respect of which such delay or omission shall take place, may cause the work so delayedï¿½ or omitted to be executed; and the expense of executing the same shall be repaidï¿½ to such persons by the said Company; and the amount of such expense shall,ï¿½ in caseï¿½ of any dispute about the same, be ascertained and recoveredï¿½ in Calcuttaï¿½ and inï¿½ any other town or place subject to the jurisdictionï¿½ of any of Her Majesty's Courts of Judicature, in the manner inï¿½ which expensesï¿½ are ascertained and recovered under Act XIV of 1856 1, andï¿½ inï¿½ anyï¿½ town or place not within the jurisdiction of any ofï¿½ Her Majesty'sï¿½ Courts, inï¿½ theï¿½ sameï¿½ mannerï¿½ asï¿½ damagesï¿½ are recoverable under this Act.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

8. Powerï¿½ to enterï¿½ buildings forï¿½ ascertaining quantityï¿½ ofï¿½ gas consumed.-

The Clerk,ï¿½ Engineer, orï¿½ other officerï¿½ duly appointed for the purposeï¿½ by theï¿½ said Company, may, at all reasonable times, enter any buildingsï¿½ or place lighted with Gas supplied by the said Company, in order to inspect the meters, fittings, and works for regulating the supply of Gas, and for the purpose of ascertaining the quantity of Gas consumed orï¿½ supplied; andï¿½ ifï¿½ anyï¿½ personï¿½ hinderï¿½ suchï¿½ officerï¿½ as aforesaid from entering and making such inspection as aforesaid at any reasonable time, he shall, for every such offence, forfeit to the said Company a sum not exceeding fifty rupees.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

9. Recoveryï¿½ of rentï¿½ due forï¿½ gas.-

If anyï¿½ person supplied with Gas, orï¿½ any personï¿½ to whomï¿½ any meter or fitting shall have been let for hire by the said Company, neglect to pay the rent due for the same to theï¿½ said Company,ï¿½ the said Company may stop the Gas from entering the premisesï¿½ of suchï¿½ person, by cutting off the service-pipes, or by such meansï¿½ as theï¿½ said Company shall think fit, and recover the rent due fromï¿½ such person,ï¿½ together withï¿½ the expenses of cutting off the Gas, by action in any Court of competent jurisdiction.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

10. Power to take away pipes when supply of gas discontinued.-

In all cases in which the said Company are authorized to cut off and takeï¿½ away theï¿½ supply ofï¿½ Gas fromï¿½ any house or building or premises under the provisions of this Act, the said Company, their agents or workmen, after giving twenty-four hours' previsions notice to the occupier, may enter into any such house, building, or premises, between the hours of nine inï¿½ the forenoon and four in the after noon, and remove and carry away anyï¿½ pipe, meter,ï¿½ fittings, orï¿½ other works, the property of the said Company.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

11. Metersï¿½ not liableï¿½ to distressï¿½ for rent, etc.-

Any meter or fitting letï¿½ for hireï¿½ by theï¿½ said Companyï¿½ shall notï¿½ be subjectï¿½ to distress forï¿½ rent orï¿½ revenue or any rate due upon the premises where the same may be used, nor be taken in execution under any process of aï¿½ Court ofï¿½ law orï¿½ equity, orï¿½ any proceeding in insolvency against theï¿½ person in whose possession the same may be.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

12. Penaltyï¿½ for fraudulentlyï¿½ using gas.-

Every person who shall lay, orï¿½ cause toï¿½ be laid,ï¿½ any pipeï¿½ to communicateï¿½ withï¿½ anyï¿½ pipe belonging toï¿½ theï¿½ saidï¿½ Company,ï¿½ withoutï¿½ theirï¿½ consent,ï¿½ orï¿½ shall fraudulently injureï¿½ any suchï¿½ meter as aforesaid, or who, in case the Gas supplied by the Company is not ascertained by meter, shall use any burner other than such as has been provided or approved of by the said Company, or of larger dimensions than he has contracted to pay for, or shall keep the lights burning for a longer time than he has contracted to payï¿½ for, orï¿½ shall otherwiseï¿½ improperly useï¿½ or burnï¿½ the Gas, or shall supply any other person with any part of the Gas supplied to him by theï¿½ said Company,ï¿½ shall forfeitï¿½ to theï¿½ said Companyï¿½ the sum of fifty rupees for

  1. Rep. by Act 16 of 1874 every such  offence, and  also the  sum of twenty rupees for every day such pipe  shall so  remain, or such works or burner shall be so used, or such excess be so committed or continued, or such supply furnished; and the  said Company may take off the Gas from the house and premises of the  person so  offending, notwithstanding  any contract  which may have been previously entered into.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

13. Penaltyï¿½ for wilfully damaging pipes.-

Every person who shall willfully remove,ï¿½ destroy, orï¿½ damage anyï¿½ pipe, pillar,ï¿½ post,ï¿½ plug, lamp, orï¿½ other workï¿½ of theï¿½ said Companyï¿½ for supplyingï¿½ Gas, or who shall willfullyï¿½ extinguish any of the public lamps or lights, or waste or improperlyï¿½ use any of the Gas supplied by the said Company, shall, for eachï¿½ such offence,ï¿½ forfeit toï¿½ theï¿½ saidï¿½ Companyï¿½ anyï¿½ sumï¿½ not exceeding fifty rupees, in addition to the amount of the damage done.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

14. Satisfactionï¿½ for accidentallyï¿½ damaging pipes.-

Every person who shallï¿½ carelessly or accidentally break, throw down, or damage any pipe, or pillar, or lamp belonging to the said Company, or under their control, shallï¿½ pay suchï¿½ sum ofï¿½ money byï¿½ way of satisfaction to theï¿½ said Companyï¿½ for theï¿½ damage done, not exceeding fifty rupees, as any Magistrate shall think reasonable.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

15. Penaltyï¿½ for causingï¿½ water toï¿½ be corrupted.ï¿½

Daily  penalty during the  continuance of  the offence.--If the said Company shall at any time  cause or  suffer to  be brought, or to flow into any stream, reservoir, aqueduct,  pond, or  place for  water, or  into  any  drain communicating therewith,  any washing  or other  substance produced in  making or  supplying Gas,  or shall willfully do any act connected with the making  or supplying of Gas, whereby the water in any such stream, reservoir, aqueduct,  pond, on  place for  water, shall be fouled, the said Company  shall forfeit for every such offence a sum not exceeding one thousand  rupees; and  they shall  forfeit an  additional sum  not exceeding five  hundred rupees  for each day during which such washing or other substance shall be brought or shall flow, or the act by which such water  shall be  found shall  continue, after  the expiration  of twenty-four hours  from the time when notice of the offence shall have been served  on the  said Company, by the person into whose water such washing or  other substance  shall be  brought or shall flow, or whose water shall  be fouled  thereby; and  such penalties  shall be paid to such last-mentioned person

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

16. Dailyï¿½ penalty duringï¿½ escape ofï¿½ Gas after notice.-

Whenever any Gasï¿½ shall escapeï¿½ from anyï¿½ pipe, laidï¿½ downï¿½ orï¿½ setï¿½ upï¿½ byï¿½ or belonging to the said Company, they shall, immediately after receiving notice thereof in writing, prevent such Gas from escaping; and in case the saidï¿½ Company shallï¿½ not,ï¿½ withinï¿½ twenty-fourï¿½ hoursï¿½ nextï¿½ after service of such notice, effectually prevent the Gas from escaping, and wholly removeï¿½ the causeï¿½ of complaint,ï¿½ theyï¿½ shallï¿½ forï¿½ everyï¿½ such offence forfeitï¿½ the sum of fifty rupees for each day during which the Gas shallï¿½ be sufferedï¿½ to escape, after the expiration of twenty four hours from the service of such notice.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

17. Penaltyï¿½ if water be fouled by Gas.-

Whenever any water shall be fouledï¿½ by theï¿½ Gas ofï¿½ the said Company, they shall forfeit to the person whose water shall be so fouled for every such offence a sum not exceeding twoï¿½ hundred rupees,ï¿½ and aï¿½ further sum,ï¿½ not exceeding one hundred rupees,ï¿½ for each day during which the offence shall continue, after theï¿½ expiration ofï¿½ twenty-four hours from the service of notice of such offence.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

18. Powerï¿½ to examine gas-pipes to ascertain cause of water being fouled.-

For theï¿½ purpose of ascertaining whether such water be fouled by theï¿½ Gas of the said Company, the person to whom the water supposed to beï¿½ fouled shallï¿½ belong, mayï¿½ dig upï¿½ the ground,ï¿½ and examine the conduits, andï¿½ works ofï¿½ the saidï¿½ Company; provided that such person, before proceeding so to dig and examine, shall give twenty-four hours' notice in writing to the said Company of the time at whichï¿½ such diggingï¿½ and examination is intended to take place, and shall giveï¿½ the likeï¿½ notice toï¿½ the personsï¿½ havingï¿½ theï¿½ controlï¿½ or management ofï¿½ the road,ï¿½ pavement, orï¿½ place where such digging is to take place,ï¿½ and theyï¿½ shall beï¿½ subject toï¿½ theï¿½ likeï¿½ obligationï¿½ of reinstating theï¿½ said roadï¿½ and pavement,ï¿½ and theï¿½ same penalties for delay, orï¿½ any nonfeasance or misfeasance therein, as are hereinbefore provided withï¿½ respect toï¿½ roads andï¿½ pavements brokenï¿½ up by the said Company, for the purpose of laying their pipes.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

19. Expensesï¿½ to abideï¿½ result ofï¿½ examination.-

If upon any such examination itï¿½ appears thatï¿½ such waterï¿½ has beenï¿½ fouled byï¿½ any Gas belongingï¿½ toï¿½ï¿½ theï¿½ saidï¿½ï¿½ Company,ï¿½ theï¿½ expensesï¿½ ofï¿½ theï¿½ digging, examination, andï¿½ repair ofï¿½ the street or place disturbed in any such examination shallï¿½ be paidï¿½ by theï¿½ said Company;ï¿½ butï¿½ ifï¿½ uponï¿½ such examination, itï¿½ appear thatï¿½ the water has not been fouled by the Gas of theï¿½ said Company,ï¿½ the person causing such examination to be made, shall payï¿½ all suchï¿½ expenses, andï¿½ shall alsoï¿½ make goodï¿½ to the said Company anyï¿½ injury whichï¿½ may beï¿½ occasioned toï¿½ their worksï¿½ by such examination.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

20. Howï¿½ expenses toï¿½ be ascertained.-

The amount of the expenses of everyï¿½ such examinationï¿½ and repair,ï¿½ and of any injury done to the said Company,ï¿½ shall, inï¿½ case of any dispute about the same, together with the costs of ascertaining and recovering the same, be ascertained and recoveredï¿½ in theï¿½ manner prescribedï¿½ forï¿½ theï¿½ ascertainmentï¿½ and recovery of expenses in section of this Act.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

21. Liabilityï¿½ to indictmentsï¿½ for nuisance.-

Nothing in this Act contained shallï¿½ prevent theï¿½ said Companyï¿½ from beingï¿½ liableï¿½ toï¿½ an indictment forï¿½ nuisance, orï¿½ to anyï¿½ other legal proceedings to which they may be liable in consequence of making or supplying Gas.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

22. Copiesï¿½ of the original Deed of Association and of all rules, etc., toï¿½ be keptï¿½ for inspectionï¿½ at theï¿½ office ofï¿½ theï¿½ Companyï¿½ in Calcutta andï¿½ in the office of the Registrar of Joint Stock Companies, or theï¿½ Keeper of the Records of the Supreme Court at Fort William.-

A copy ofï¿½ the originalï¿½ Deed of Association of the said Company, and ofï¿½ everyï¿½ otherï¿½ï¿½ instrumentï¿½ registeredï¿½ underï¿½ theï¿½ saidï¿½ "Jointï¿½ Stock Companies' Act,ï¿½ 1856", asï¿½ constituting theï¿½ Regulations ofï¿½ the said Company, andï¿½ a copyï¿½ of every special resolution of a General Meeting whereby anyï¿½ change shallï¿½ have been,ï¿½ or at any time shall be made in the Regulationsï¿½ of theï¿½ said Company,ï¿½ shall be kept at the office of the saidï¿½ Companyï¿½ inï¿½ Calcutta,ï¿½ andï¿½ shallï¿½ thereï¿½ beï¿½ openï¿½ toï¿½ the inspection ofï¿½ all personsï¿½ during theï¿½ usual hours of business of the said office;ï¿½ and aï¿½ copy of such original Deed of Association, and of every otherï¿½ such instrument,ï¿½ andï¿½ ofï¿½ everyï¿½ specialï¿½ resolutionï¿½ as aforesaid, shallï¿½ also beï¿½ deposited by the said Company as soon as it can beï¿½ done after the passing of this Act, or after the making of any such specialï¿½ resolution hereafterï¿½ to beï¿½ made, inï¿½ the office of the Registrar ofï¿½ Joint Stockï¿½ Companies, or, if there be no such officer, in theï¿½ office ofï¿½ the Keeperï¿½ of theï¿½ Records of the Supreme Court of Judicature atï¿½ Fort Williamï¿½ 1*,ï¿½ andï¿½ï¿½ shallï¿½ thereï¿½ be filed; and an examined copyï¿½ of anyï¿½ such filedï¿½ copy as aforesaid, certified by and under theï¿½ hand ofï¿½ the Registrarï¿½ of Joint Stock Companies, or of the Keeper ofï¿½ the Recordsï¿½ of theï¿½ said Supremeï¿½ Court, shall be good and sufficient evidence of each such original Deed, instrument, or special resolution, in all actions, suits, and proceedings whatsoever, whether civil orï¿½ criminal, toï¿½ be hadï¿½ in anyï¿½ Court of Justice or before any Magistrate, or Revenue or other officer, and whether acting judicially or inï¿½ any proceedingsï¿½ preliminary toï¿½ a judicial inquiry, throughout the territories of the East India Company.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

23. Serviceï¿½ of process.-

All services of manse or other process, and allï¿½ notices whatsoeverï¿½ which, byï¿½ law orï¿½ by the practice of any Court whereinï¿½ the saidï¿½ Company shall sue or be sued, are required to be made, served, and given, in addition to all ways and means by which the same may otherwise be legally made, and given, by leaving the same addressed toï¿½ the Managingï¿½ Agent of the said Company at the office in Calcutta of the said company.

  1. Now High Court at Calcutta

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

24. Recoveryï¿½ of penalties,ï¿½ etc.-

All penaltiesï¿½ and forfeitures imposed byï¿½ this Act,ï¿½ and allï¿½ damages andï¿½ expenses theï¿½ recovery of which isï¿½ not speciallyï¿½ provided for,ï¿½ may beï¿½ recoveredï¿½ byï¿½ summary proceeding before a Magistrate.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

25. Levyï¿½ by distress.-

Allï¿½ penalties, forfeitures, damages, and expenses adjudgedï¿½ due underï¿½ this Act, if the amount be not otherwise paid, mayï¿½ be levied by distress and sale of the goods and chattels of the partyï¿½ liable toï¿½ pay the same, and the over plus arising from such goods and chattels, afterï¿½ satisfying such amount and the expenses of the distressï¿½ and sale, shall be returned on demand to the party whose goods shall have been distained; or instead of proceeding by distress and sale,ï¿½ or inï¿½ case ofï¿½ failure to realise by distress the whole or any partï¿½ of anyï¿½ penalties, forfeitures, damages, or expenses imposed or incurred under the provisions of this Act, the person claiming such penalty, forfeiture, damage, or expenses, may sue the person liable to pay the same in any Court of competent jurisdiction.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

26. Noï¿½ distress unlawfulï¿½ for wantï¿½ of form,ï¿½ etc.-

Noï¿½ distress levied byï¿½ virtue ofï¿½ this Act shall be deemed unlawful, nor shall any party making the same be deemed a trespasser, on account of any defect or wantï¿½ of formï¿½ in theï¿½ summons, conviction, warrant of distress, or other proceedingï¿½ relating thereto, nor shall any such party be deemed a trespasserï¿½ abs initialï¿½ on accountï¿½ ofï¿½ anyï¿½ irregularityï¿½ afterwards committed byï¿½ him; butï¿½ all personsï¿½ aggrieved by any irregularity may recover fullï¿½ satisfaction forï¿½ the specialï¿½ damage inï¿½ anyï¿½ Courtï¿½ of competent jurisdiction.

The Oriental Gas Company Act No. 1857

27. Interpretation.-

Theï¿½ following words and expressions used in this Act shall have the meanings hereby assigned to them, unless there be somethingï¿½ in the subject or context repugnant to such construction (that is to say):-

Words importing the singular number only shall include the plural number; andï¿½ words importing the plural number only shall include also the singular number. Words importing the masculine gender shall include females. The wordï¿½ "person" shall include a corporation, whether aggregate or sole. The wordï¿½ "street" shallï¿½ include anyï¿½ square, court,ï¿½ orï¿½ alley, highway, lane, road, thoroughfare, or public passage or place. The word "Magistrate" shall include any Magistrate of Police, and any joint Magistrate or other person lawfully exercising the powers of Magistrate, actingï¿½ at orï¿½ for theï¿½ place or district where the matter requiring the cognizance of any such Magistrate arises.
